description: This study explores the lived experiences of family caregivers — what the role involves day-to-day, how it affects your wellbeing and relationships, and what support would make a real difference. Your perspective will help us better understand and address the needs of caregivers. There are no right or wrong answers — please share as much or as little as you feel comfortable with.

topics.guiding_question: Can you tell me a little about the person you care for and how you came to take on that role?
objectives: Understand who the participant cares for and the nature of the care relationship
Explore how the participant came into the caregiving role (planned vs. unexpected)
Establish baseline context about the duration, intensity, and type of caregiving

topics.guiding_question: Walk me through what a typical day looks like for you as a caregiver. What does that feel like?
objectives: Understand the concrete day-to-day tasks and responsibilities involved in caregiving
Explore the emotional texture of the participant's daily caregiving experience
Identify moments of stress, reward, or ambiguity in the daily routine

topics.guiding_question: Caregiving can affect people in many ways. How has this role affected you personally — your emotions, your relationships, your sense of self?
objectives: Understand the emotional toll or rewards of caregiving on the participant
Explore how caregiving has affected relationships with family, friends, and partners
Identify shifts in the participant's identity, priorities, or sense of purpose

topics.guiding_question: What kinds of support, if any, have you been able to rely on — whether from family, friends, professionals, or services?
objectives: Understand what formal and informal support the participant currently has access to
Explore how the participant feels about the adequacy and quality of that support
Identify gaps between what support exists and what the participant actually needs

topics.guiding_question: What has been the hardest part of caregiving that you feel hasn't been well understood or addressed by others?
objectives: Surface the participant's most pressing unmet needs as a caregiver
Understand how the participant experiences being misunderstood or unsupported
Identify systemic, social, or personal barriers to getting adequate help

topics.guiding_question: Is there anything about your experience as a caregiver that you feel is important for others to understand, that we haven't touched on yet?
objectives: Give the participant space to share any perspectives not yet captured
Invite reflection on what the participant most wants others to understand
Close the interview respectfully and affirm the value of their contribution
